control_name: Pseudonymisation and encryption of personal data
notes: AES-256-GCM encryption. Identity tag tokenization via BLAKE3 with per-tenant salt. Encryption context hashing prevents PII leakage in headers
Confidentiality: AES-256-GCM. Integrity: GCM authentication tag. Availability: HA in commercial edition. Resilience: key rotation and versioning

control_name: Ability to restore availability and access to data
notes: Key versioning ensures old ciphertexts remain decryptable. HA/DR depends on deployment (commercial:keyrack-ha). Metadata backup is operational

control_name: Process for regularly testing, assessing, evaluating security
notes: Metrics and audit trail support assessment. No built-in penetration testing or assessment automation
Pluggable provider architecture: software for low-risk, HSM for high-risk. PDP authorization scales with policy complexity
destroy_key() with cascade-disable. Destroying a root key permanently renders all data in the hierarchy unrecoverable. HSM material physically erased
Key destruction audit events emitted via NATS. Downstream processors can subscribe. No built-in notification protocol to data processors
Legal obligations to retain data are organizational decisions; KeyRack provides disable (preserve decrypt) vs destroy (irreversible)
Mandatory encryption — no unencrypted path. Fail-closed authorization. Encryption context binding prevents misuse. Sensitive<T> prevents accidental exposure
Default deny (PDP fail-closed). Identity tags excluded from responses by default. Encryption context hashed before storage. No opt-out from authorization
DPIA template available in commercial compliance package. FOSS users need to create their own DPIA
Encrypted data at rest reduces breach notification obligations under Art 34(3)(a). Crypto-shredding provides additional mitigation
Destruction events logged in audit trail. No signed destruction certificate for evidentiary purposes. Recommended enhancement
Key metadata stored in postgres/sqlite. Data residency depends on where the database is deployed — operational concern
Encryption context is BLAKE3-hashed before storage in ciphertext header. Pre-image not recoverable. Small context spaces may allow brute-force — document in DPIA
Attribute-based key hierarchy provides logical tenant isolation. PDP enforces tenant boundaries. Identity tags tokenized with per-tenant salt
